decisions.detail: Users will log in exclusively via SSO through the external Portal.

evidence.quote: Users will log in exclusively via SSO through the external Portal.

decisions.title: System will not implement its own login or registration
decisions.detail: This system will not implement its own login or registration functionality.

decisions.title: Photo upload is a core V1 feature
decisions.detail: Photo upload is one of the two main features prioritized for V1.

decisions.title: Output modeling is a core V1 feature
decisions.detail: Output modeling is one of the two main features prioritized for V1.

decisions.title: Sidebar must distinguish between 'Album' and 'Project' folder types
decisions.detail: The sidebar needs to distinguish between two folder types: 'Album' for managing photos and 'Project' for managing modeling data.

evidence.quote: The sidebar needs to distinguish between two folder types — 'Album' and 'Project' — managing photos and modeling data separately
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Album must support adding photos
decisions.detail: The Album interface must provide functionality for adding photos.

evidence.quote: The Album must support adding, deleting, and searching photos
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Album must support deleting photos
decisions.detail: The Album interface must provide functionality for deleting photos.

evidence.quote: The Album must support adding, deleting, and searching photos
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Album must support searching photos
decisions.detail: The Album interface must provide functionality for searching photos.

evidence.quote: The Album must support adding, deleting, and searching photos
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Modeling progress must be shown as a progress bar
decisions.detail: Modeling progress must be displayed visually as a progress bar.

evidence.quote: Modeling progress must be shown as a progress bar, displaying the status of each stage (AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation, etc.)
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Modeling progress bar must display status of each stage
decisions.detail: The modeling progress bar must show the status of individual stages, such as AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation.

evidence.quote: Modeling progress must be shown as a progress bar, displaying the status of each stage (AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation, etc.)

decisions.title: Users can select photos from photo list to import into project folder when creating a project
decisions.detail: When creating a new project, users must be able to select existing photos from their photo list to include in the project folder.

evidence.quote: When creating a project, users can select photos from the photo list to import into the project folder
evidence.approximate_location: I. Core Features (V1 Implementation Scope) - Interface & Interaction

decisions.title: Project sharing is a future feature
decisions.detail: The ability to share a project folder with other users for joint viewing of modeling results is planned for a future version.

evidence.quote: Project sharing: A project folder can designate other users to jointly view the modeling results

decisions.title: Albums are private and do not support multi-user access (future)
decisions.detail: In future versions, Albums will remain private to the owner only and will not support multi-user access.

evidence.quote: Albums are private to the owner only and do not support multi-user access

decisions.title: Resumable uploads are a future feature
decisions.detail: Future versions will support resumable uploads, preserving upload progress via a caching mechanism if an upload is interrupted.

evidence.quote: Resumable uploads: If an upload is interrupted, upload progress is preserved via a caching mechanism, supporting resume-from-breakpoint

decisions.title: Storage quota management is a future feature
decisions.detail: Future versions will include storage quota management, displaying used and remaining storage on the user's personal page.

evidence.quote: Storage quota management: The user's personal page displays used and remaining storage

decisions.title: Photo-upload limit can be dynamically adjusted (future)
decisions.detail: In future versions, each user's photo-upload limit can be dynamically adjusted based on system settings and permissions.

evidence.quote: each user's photo-upload limit can be dynamically adjusted based on system settings and permissions

decisions.title: AT error handling with parameter adjustment is a future feature
decisions.detail: Future versions will provide a mechanism to adjust parameters and re-run AT computation if it fails.

evidence.quote: AT error handling: When AT computation fails, provide a mechanism to adjust parameters and re-run

decisions.title: Expert mode is a future feature
decisions.detail: Expert Mode, allowing freely adjustable parameters after photo upload, is planned for a future version.

evidence.quote: Expert mode: After photo upload, distinguish between 'Normal Mode' (default parameters) and 'Expert Mode' (freely adjustable parameters)

decisions.title: V1 will implement Normal Mode only
decisions.detail: The initial V1 implementation will only include 'Normal Mode' with default parameters.

evidence.quote: V1 will implement Normal Mode only

implementation_scope: Integrate with external SSO Portal for user authentication and authorization.
Develop photo management module (Album folder type) supporting adding, deleting, and searching photos.
Develop output modeling module (Project folder type) including initiating modeling processes and displaying progress.
Implement sidebar navigation UI component to visually distinguish 'Album' and 'Project' folder types.
Implement project creation workflow allowing users to select and import existing photos from their Album into a Project folder.
Develop modeling progress bar UI component capable of displaying overall progress and granular status for each modeling stage (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation).
Develop backend services for photo storage, retrieval, and management (add, delete, search).
Develop backend services for modeling data storage and management.
Implement 'Normal Mode' for the modeling process, utilizing default parameters.

constraints.text: Authentication and user registration must be handled exclusively via an external SSO Portal; no internal login or registration functionality will be developed.

architectural_decisions.text: The modeling process for V1 will operate solely in 'Normal Mode' with predefined default parameters, excluding any 'Expert Mode' functionality.

open_technical_questions: What specific SSO protocol (e.g., OIDC, SAML, custom API) is mandated for integration with the external Portal?
What are the technical specifications for photo uploads (e.g., maximum file size, supported image formats, required metadata, acceptable latency)?
What database or storage solution will be used for storing photos and modeling output data?
What are the precise API contracts and communication mechanisms for pushing or pulling modeling progress updates to the UI in real-time?
What search technology or indexing strategy will be implemented for efficient photo searching within Albums?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user is not logged in, When attempting to access the system, Then the user is redirected to the external Portal for SSO login.
Given a user is successfully logged in via SSO, When the user accesses the system, Then the system displays the main application interface.
Given the system is running, When a user attempts to find an internal login or registration page, Then no such functionality is present or accessible within the system itself.
Given the main application interface is displayed, When the sidebar is visible, Then the sidebar prominently displays distinct 'Album' and 'Project' folder types.
Given an 'Album' folder is selected, When the user interacts with it, Then the interface allows for photo management functionalities.
Given a user is in an 'Album' folder, When the user attempts to add a photo, Then the system provides an interface to add photos.
Given a user is in an 'Album' folder containing photos, When the user attempts to delete a photo, Then the system provides an interface to delete photos.
Given a user is in an 'Album' folder containing photos, When the user attempts to search for photos, Then the system provides a search interface for photos.
Given a 'Project' folder is selected, When the user interacts with it, Then the interface allows for modeling data management functionalities.
Given a modeling process is initiated, When the process is active, Then a visual progress bar is displayed.
Given a modeling process is active, When the progress bar is displayed, Then it shows the status of individual stages (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation).
Given a user is creating a new 'Project', When prompted to add photos, Then the user can select one or more existing photos from their 'Album' photo list.
Given a user is initiating an output modeling process, When prompted for parameters, Then only 'Normal Mode' with default parameters is available.
Given a user is initiating an output modeling process, When attempting to adjust parameters, Then no options for freely adjustable parameters are presented.
test_scenarios: Verify successful login to the system via the external SSO portal.
Attempt to access the system directly without going through the SSO portal and confirm redirection.
Attempt to locate any internal login, registration, or password reset functionality within the system and confirm its absence.
Navigate to the sidebar and confirm that both 'Album' and 'Project' folder types are clearly distinguishable.
Create a new 'Album' and successfully upload a single photo.
Create a new 'Album' and successfully upload multiple photos.
Select an existing photo in an 'Album' and successfully delete it.
Select multiple existing photos in an 'Album' and successfully delete them.
Use the photo search functionality in an 'Album' to find an existing photo by its name.
Use the photo search functionality in an 'Album' to search for a non-existent photo and confirm appropriate feedback.
Initiate an output modeling process and observe the progress bar, verifying it displays distinct stages and updates visually.
Create a new 'Project' and select several photos from an existing 'Album' to import into it. Confirm photos are associated with the project.
Create a new 'Project' without selecting any photos and verify the project creation is successful and handles the empty photo list gracefully.
Attempt to access advanced modeling parameters or 'Expert Mode' settings and confirm they are not present or accessible in V1.
edge_cases: Login with a user whose SSO session has expired and verify correct re-authentication flow.
Attempt to upload a very large photo file (e.g., >100MB) to an 'Album'.
Attempt to upload a photo with an unsupported file type (e.g., .doc, .exe) to an 'Album'.
Attempt to delete all photos from an 'Album' and verify the album state.
Search for photos using special characters or an empty string in the search bar.
Initiate a modeling job that is expected to fail (if applicable) and observe progress bar behavior during failure.
Observe modeling progress for a very short duration job and a very long duration job.
Test creating a 'Project' with a maximum number of photos allowed (if a limit is defined).
Rapidly add and delete photos from an album concurrently.
unresolved_behaviors: What is the expected behavior if SSO login fails due to an unauthorized user or other SSO-related errors? (PM/QA)
What specific photo file types are supported for upload to an 'Album'? (PM/QA)
Is there a maximum file size for photo uploads to an 'Album'? If so, what is it, and what is the error message for exceeding it? (PM/QA)
What specific criteria are supported for photo searching within an 'Album' (e.g., filename, metadata, tags, capture date)? (PM/QA)
What are all the distinct stages that the modeling progress bar should display? (PM/QA)
What is the expected behavior and UI feedback if a specific stage of the modeling process fails? (PM/QA)
What is the expected UI behavior for the progress bar (e.g., color changes, animation, specific text updates for completion/failure/paused states)? (PM/QA)

decision_history: **V1 Login Strategy**: It has been confirmed that users will log in exclusively via SSO through an external Portal, and this system will not implement its own login or registration functionality. This simplifies V1 development by offloading authentication (D001, D002).
**V1 Core Features**: Photo upload and Output modeling are the two main features prioritized and confirmed for the V1 release, serving as the foundational capabilities (D003, D004).

**V1 User Interface Decisions**: The sidebar in V1 must clearly distinguish between 'Album' type folders (for managing photos) and 'Project' type folders (for managing modeling data) to improve organization (D005).
**V1 Album Functionality**: The Album interface must support core photo management actions: adding, deleting, and searching photos (D006, D007, D008).

**V1 Modeling Progress Display**: Modeling progress will be visually represented by a progress bar that displays the status of individual stages, such as AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation, to provide user feedback (D009, D010).

**V1 Project Creation Workflow**: When creating a new project, users must be able to select existing photos from their photo list to import into the project folder, streamlining the project setup process (D011).

**V1 Scope Limitation - Normal Mode**: For V1, the system will only implement 'Normal Mode' with default parameters for photo processing. 'Expert Mode', which allows for freely adjustable parameters, is explicitly deferred to a future version (D019, D018).

**Future Features (Post-V1)**: Several features are explicitly deferred to future versions, including Project sharing (D012), multi-user access for Albums (Albums remain private) (D013), resumable uploads (D014), storage quota management (D015), dynamically adjustable photo-upload limits (D016), and AT error handling with parameter adjustment (D017). This clarifies V1 scope to focus on core capabilities.
dependencies: **External SSO Portal**: The entire V1 login and user authentication process is critically dependent on the functionality, reliability, and API of an external SSO Portal. This is a hard dependency for user access (D001, D002).

**Internal Album Management**: The 'Project' creation workflow's ability to allow users to 'select photos from the photo list' (D011) implies a dependency on the 'Album' functionality (add, delete, search photos - D006, D007, D008) being fully functional and providing an accessible photo repository.

**Modeling Backend Services (Inferred)**: The core V1 feature of 'Output modeling' (D004) and the granular 'Modeling progress bar' (D009, D010) that displays statuses for stages like 'AT computing', 'point cloud generation', and 'model generation' infer a dependency on underlying, robust backend processing services capable of executing these complex computational tasks.
risks: **External SSO Integration Risk**: The reliance on an external SSO Portal for all login (D001) introduces a significant risk. Any delays, API inconsistencies, or lack of responsiveness from the external Portal team could directly block V1 launch. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High).

**Undefined Scope for Core Features**: While 'Photo upload' and 'Output modeling' are confirmed V1 features (D003, D004), the notes lack detailed scope definitions, acceptance criteria, or functional breakdowns. This poses a high risk for scope creep, rework, and potential timeline overruns if not clarified early. (Likelihood: High, Impact: High).

**Unmanaged User Expectations for Advanced Features**: Several features, including 'Expert Mode' (D018, D019), 'Project Sharing' (D012), and 'Resumable Uploads' (D014), have been explicitly deferred. There's a risk of user dissatisfaction or negative feedback if these are highly anticipated for V1 and their absence is not well-communicated. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium).

**Performance & Scalability of Modeling Backend (Inferred)**: The requirement for 'Output modeling' and detailed progress tracking (D004, D009, D010) implies significant computational resources. Without further detail, there's a risk that the underlying modeling backend's performance and scalability may not meet user expectations or V1 load requirements. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High).
alignment_gaps: **SSO Integration Details with External Team**: While SSO via an external Portal is a firm decision (D001), the meeting notes do not specify the exact technical integration protocol, required identity attributes, user provisioning flows, or error handling mechanisms. This requires clear alignment with both the external Portal team and internal technical leads.

**'Photo List' Definition & Scope**: The ability for users to 'select photos from the photo list' when creating a project (D011) needs clearer definition. It's unclear if this 'photo list' is simply an aggregation of all user-uploaded photos across 'Albums', or a distinct entity. Potential for misunderstanding regarding data organization and access.

**Definition of 'Normal Mode' Parameters**: For V1, only 'Normal Mode' is to be implemented (D019). The specific default parameters for this mode and how they differ from what users might expect from 'Expert Mode' (D018) require clear definition and stakeholder agreement to prevent future conflicts.
open_questions: What are the detailed functional and non-functional requirements for the SSO integration with the external Portal, including specific user attributes needed, session management, and expected error handling? (Impact: High, Owned by: PM/QA)

Who are the key contacts for the external SSO Portal team, and what is their expected availability and process for technical consultation and issue resolution during V1 development? (Impact: High, Owned by: PM)
What are the specific acceptance criteria, user stories, and performance expectations for the 'Photo Upload' and 'Output Modeling' core V1 features? (Impact: High, Owned by: PM/QA)

Please clarify the exact definition of the 'photo list' from which users select photos for projects: Is it all photos uploaded, photos within specific albums, or a separate concept? How does this interact with Album privacy (D013)? (Impact: Medium, Owned by: PM/QA)

system_requirements: The system SHALL integrate with an external Portal for user authentication via Single Sign-On (SSO).
The system MUST NOT implement its own user login or registration functionality.
The system SHALL provide functionality for users to upload photos.
The system SHALL provide functionality for users to perform output modeling.
The system user interface SHALL display a sidebar that visually distinguishes between 'Album' folder types (for managing photos) and 'Project' folder types (for managing modeling data).
The system SHALL enable users to add photos to an 'Album' folder.
The system SHALL enable users to delete photos from an 'Album' folder.
The system SHALL enable users to search for photos within an 'Album' folder.
The system user interface SHALL display the progress of modeling operations as a visual progress bar.
The modeling progress bar SHALL display the status of individual stages, including but not limited to AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation.
The system SHALL allow users to select existing photos from their personal photo list for inclusion in a new 'Project' folder during its creation.
Albums MUST be private to the owner only and MUST NOT support multi-user access for V1 (inferred - needs confirmation).
The system SHALL utilize default parameters for all modeling operations in V1 (inferred from D019).
data_flows: User identity and authentication status flow from the External SSO Portal to this system.
Photos uploaded by users flow from the client application to this system's photo storage component.
Photos are selected and imported (logically or physically) from an 'Album' folder into a 'Project' folder within the system.
Modeling data/instructions flow from the user interface to a backend modeling processing component.
Modeling progress status updates (e.g., percentage complete, current stage status) flow from the backend modeling processing component to the user interface.
interface_contracts: SSO Integration Contract: The system SHALL integrate with the external Portal using a standard SSO protocol (e.g., OIDC, SAML) to receive authenticated user identity. Specific endpoints, expected claims, and session management details must be defined.
Photo Upload API: The system SHALL expose an API endpoint for clients to upload photo files. This contract must define acceptable file formats, maximum file sizes, and required metadata.

Photo Management API: The system SHALL expose API endpoints for adding, deleting, and searching photos within an 'Album' folder. This includes specifications for photo identification, deletion confirmation, and search criteria parameters.

Project Creation/Photo Import API: The system SHALL expose an API endpoint for creating a 'Project' folder, accepting parameters for project metadata and a list of existing photo identifiers to be associated with the project.

Modeling Progress Status API: The system SHALL expose an API endpoint for clients to query the status of ongoing modeling jobs, providing progress (e.g., percentage complete) and detailed status of each stage (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation).

constraints.text: The system is constrained from developing its own login or registration functionality and must exclusively rely on an external Portal for user authentication.

architectural_decisions.text: The V1 system implementation will only support 'Normal Mode' for modeling, which means modeling operations will utilize default parameters without user-adjustable options.

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific technical details (protocol, endpoints, required claims, error handling) for the SSO integration with the external Portal?
What are the precise technical requirements for photo uploads, including supported file types, maximum file dimensions, maximum file size per photo, and total photo limits for an album/project?

What are all the distinct stages of the modeling process that need to be tracked and displayed on the progress bar, beyond those explicitly mentioned (AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation)?
What is the underlying data model for 'Album' and 'Project' folder types, including attributes, relationships, and permissions?

What is the system's strategy for importing photos into a project folder from an existing photo list: are photos copied, symlinked, or referenced? What are the implications for photo deletion or modification in the source album?

What are the specific types of outputs expected from the 'Output modeling' functionality (e.g., 3D mesh, point cloud, orthophoto, DSM, texture map), and their respective data formats and quality requirements?
What are the search criteria and expected performance for 'searching photos' functionality within an Album (e.g., by name, tags, date, metadata)?

stakeholder.business_summary: The initial version (V1) of our new system will focus on two core functionalities: managing photo uploads and generating 3D models. Users will log in seamlessly using our existing external portal, eliminating the need for separate registration within this system. We have prioritized a clear and simple user experience for the first release, deferring more advanced features to future development.
key_impact: Users will experience streamlined access, leveraging their existing external login without a new registration process.
The core value proposition of photo management and 3D model generation will be delivered in the first release, providing immediate utility.
The V1 user experience will be kept simple with default settings for 3D modeling, reducing initial complexity for users.
Future enhancements such as project sharing, advanced upload options, and customizable modeling parameters have been clearly planned for subsequent versions, ensuring a focused V1 launch.
major_unresolved_questions: No major unresolved questions are currently blocking the V1 project, as the core functionalities and scope have been clearly defined and confirmed.

issues.description: The requirement 'The system SHALL utilize default parameters for all modeling operations in V1 (inferred from D019)' incorrectly states it is 'inferred'. Decision D019 explicitly confirms this as a fact ('V1 will implement Normal Mode only'), not an inference.
issues.suggestion: Correct the statement to reflect that this is a confirmed requirement, not an inferred one, by removing '(inferred from D019)' or stating it is 'confirmed' instead of 'inferred'.

issues.description: The statement in 'major_unresolved_questions' that 'No major unresolved questions are currently blocking the V1 project' contradicts the 'PM View' and 'Systems Analysis View', both of which list several significant open questions and alignment gaps.
issues.suggestion: Revise the 'major_unresolved_questions' in the Stakeholder View to acknowledge the existing open questions/alignment gaps from other views, or clarify that 'major' refers to strategic, high-level blockers, not detailed technical points.

issues.description: The requirement 'Albums MUST be private to the owner only and MUST NOT support multi-user access for V1 (inferred - needs confirmation)' unnecessarily adds 'inferred - needs confirmation'. Decision D013 already confirms this as a future state, implying it's the default/current state for V1 by not being a feature to be built.
issues.suggestion: Consider removing '(inferred - needs confirmation)' as D013 provides sufficient confidence on this aspect of Albums, even if it is listed under future planning.

review.reviewer_notes: The report generally captures the extracted decisions well across roles. However, there is a critical hallucination in the Systems Analysis view regarding an 'inferred' vs. 'confirmed' decision, and a notable contradiction in the Stakeholder view regarding the presence of open questions when compared to other role outputs. These issues need to be addressed before publishing.
